patterns.name: Sliding Window
hints: Can you avoid recomputing the whole window every time you move it?
What should happen to the window boundaries as you scan left to right?
What condition tells you when to shrink the window versus grow it?

patterns.name: Two Pointers
hints: If the array is sorted, what does moving one pointer inward tell you?
Where should your two pointers start?
What decides whether you move the left pointer, the right pointer, or both?

patterns.name: Fast & Slow Pointers
hints: What happens if one pointer moves twice as fast as the other?
Will the fast pointer ever lap the slow pointer if there's a cycle?
How would you find the cycle's starting point once a meeting point is found?

patterns.name: Prefix Sum
hints: Could you precompute cumulative sums to answer range queries instantly?
How would you get the sum of a subarray using two prefix sums?
What's the prefix sum at index -1 conceptually?

patterns.name: Binary Search
hints: What property of the array lets you eliminate half the search space each step?
How do you decide whether to search the left or right half?
What's your loop's termination condition?

patterns.name: Merge / Quick Sort
hints: How would you split the problem into smaller subproblems?
What's the base case for your recursion?
How do you combine two sorted halves efficiently?

patterns.name: Counting Sort
hints: What if you counted occurrences of each value instead of comparing elements?
How does the range of input values affect your space usage?
How would you reconstruct the sorted array from counts?

patterns.name: BFS & DFS Tracing
hints: Should you explore level by level, or go as deep as possible first?
How do you avoid revisiting the same node?
What data structure naturally enforces the order you want to visit nodes?

patterns.name: Kruskal & Prim MST
hints: How do you avoid creating a cycle while connecting components?
Should you process edges by weight, or grow outward from a node?
What structure helps you check efficiently if two nodes are already connected?

patterns.name: Topological Sort
hints: How do you know a node is safe to process - what must happen first?
What does it mean if you can't find any node with zero incoming edges?
Could counting incoming edges per node help you decide processing order?

patterns.name: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
hints: What data structure helps you always process the closest unvisited node first?
How would you track the shortest known distance to each node?
What happens when you reach a node you've already finalized?

patterns.name: Union-Find Structural Maps
hints: How would you quickly check if two elements belong to the same group?
What happens to lookup speed if you always point to a representative root?
Could path compression speed up future queries?

patterns.name: Tree Traversals & LCA
hints: What order should you visit root, left, and right in for your goal?
How would recursion naturally handle going deeper into the tree?
If two nodes split at a certain point, what does that point represent?

patterns.name: Linked List Mutation
hints: How many pointers do you need to track while reversing links?
What happens to the next pointer before you reassign it?
Do you need a dummy head node to simplify edge cases?

patterns.name: Memoization / Tabulation
hints: Are you solving the same subproblem more than once?
Could you cache results instead of recomputing them?
What's the smallest subproblem you can solve directly?

patterns.name: 0/1 & Unbounded Knapsack
hints: For each item, what two choices do you have?
Can an item be reused, or only picked once?
How would you build up the answer from smaller capacities?

patterns.name: Longest Common Subsequence
hints: What do you do when characters at the current positions match?
What do you do when they don't match?
Could a 2D table track the best answer for every prefix pair?

patterns.name: Backtracking Exploration
hints: What choice are you making at each step of the recursion?
When should you undo a choice and try another?
What condition tells you a partial solution is invalid early?

patterns.name: Greedy Optimizations
hints: What local choice seems best at each step?
Does sorting the input first reveal an obvious greedy order?
Can you prove the greedy choice never leads to a worse global outcome?

patterns.name: Monotonic Stacks / Queues
hints: What should you do when the current element breaks the stack's order?
Should the stack stay increasing or decreasing for this problem?
What does popping from the stack tell you about that popped element?

patterns.name: Trie Structure Matching
hints: How would you share common prefixes between words in your structure?
What marks the end of a valid word in your tree?
How would you search for all words starting with a given prefix?
